comment: The adapter device — its own address window. Every node is an addressed register; every op is a cell between registers. The clock is a power-bit-gated dff counter; the pacer comparator is a gate netlist (bit-serial), not a C compare. Selection is mask algebra. No if/switch/?: anywhere in the generated data path.

config_nodes.name: ADP_POWER
config_nodes.comment: power bit — set once; the clock self-runs while 1

config_nodes.name: SPEED
config_nodes.comment: static per-adapter config (connector/init, like pip): real-world-clock rate scale; ADP_CLK advances SPEED units/tick. Default 1 = 1 real-world unit/tick. Local to the adapter; never touches a system clock; never advanced by source timestamps.

dff_nodes.name: ADP_CLK
dff_nodes.comment: real-world-time counter; +1 per tick while powered

dff_nodes.name: WIRE_BID_PX
dff_nodes.comment: latched bid price on the wire (en=DUE)

dff_nodes.name: WIRE_ASK_PX
dff_nodes.comment: latched ask price on the wire (en=DUE)

dff_nodes.name: WIRE_TIME
dff_nodes.comment: latched source time on the wire (en=DUE)

dff_nodes.name: WIRE_SEQ
dff_nodes.comment: latched source seq on the wire (en=DUE)

dff_nodes.name: WIRE_VALID
dff_nodes.comment: 1 when a packet was emitted this tick (en=ADP_POWER, d=DUE)

comb_nodes.name: TS_LE_NOW
comb_nodes.comment: gate-netlist comparator: 1 (bit0) iff RX_TS <= ADP_CLK now

comb_nodes.name: DUE
comb_nodes.comment: this record's real-world time has arrived and a record exists

comb_nodes.name: CLK_STEP
comb_nodes.comment: clock advance amount this tick: SPEED while powered, 0 while off (register-backed power-gate, no literal). ADP_CLK += CLK_STEP.

clock.comment: ADP_CLK += CLK_STEP every tick (CLK_STEP = SPEED while ADP_POWER bit0 == 1, else 0). Free-running, real-world rate scaled by SPEED.

pos.comment: POS += DUE (0 or 1) each tick — consumes the record on emit

writeout.comment: gated dffs (en=DUE) form the adapter's registered output flops holding the price-only packet (the record + static config); WIRE_VALID <= DUE (en=ADP_POWER). These flops are the bus DRIVER — their values are deposited into the wire bus window (see deposit), where the WIRE_* lanes actually live and the NIC samples them.

deposit.comment: The adapter DRIVES the passive wire bus: in the WRITE phase it deposits its registered output flops directly into the wire window (INGRESS_FLOW.md s2/s3). The WIRE_* lanes live in the wire, not in a private copy the NIC reaches into; the adapter is the bus's sole writer. Branchless: plain register writes of already-computed gated values. Target lane names are the wire window's (wire_gen.h, the wire's canonical generated seam map — single source ../wire/wire.net.json).

display_ring.comment: display-out lanes: a non-blocking snapshot ring the device appends to on each emit (en=DUE), wrapping at depth (slot = emit_cnt & (depth-1), a power-of-two mask cell — no %), so the newest `depth` emits are retained. An external display scans raw off-clock and windows to the most-recent `depth`. depth MUST stay a power of two for the mask. ADP_CLK_AT_EMIT records now at emit to prove now==timestamp. This is a display surface (addressed registers), not data-path logic.
